The USD/JPY pair is down 0.21%, trading at 159.96.

data.title: ICE agents could stay at airports despite TSA pay resuming. Border czar Tom Homan says shortages may keep them there.
data.description: On Monday, transportation safety officers are scheduled to receive their payments. However, Tom Homan, the White House's border czar, mentioned that ICE agents might remain deployed in areas experiencing staffing deficiencies.

data.title: Is the minimum of two overnight air traffic controllers enough? LaGuardia accident raises concerns.
data.description: Questions have been raised following an accident at LaGuardia Airport regarding the adequacy of the minimum requirement of having two air traffic controllers on overnight shifts. The incident has prompted concerns about whether this standard is enough to ensure air traffic safety during late-night hours at airports. The issue highlights the importance of maintaining proper staffing levels to prevent potential risks and ensure the smooth operation of air traffic control.
The US president told the FT he is considering seizing Kharg Island, the hub through which most of Iran’s oil is exported.

Donald Trump claimed that Cuba will collapse soon.
Donald Trump indicated that a deal with Iran is within reach and may be finalized in the near term.

Ueda noted that monetary policy decisions will consider the potential impact of currency and market fluctuations on maintaining the 2% inflation target.
Boj Ueda said foreign exchange fluctuations may influence underlying inflation by shaping inflation expectations.
Ueda noted that with firms becoming more proactive on pricing and wages, FX movements are having a larger effect on prices.
Ueda stated that FX developments will be watched carefully.
Boj Ueda stated that exchange rate shifts are among the major drivers affecting Japan’s economy and inflation dynamics.

data.title: Insilico Medicine Announces Global R&D Collaboration with Lilly
data.description: Insilico Medicine, a biotech company based in Cambridge, Massachusetts, has recently secured an exclusive global license for a series of programs covering various medical fields through AI-powered collaboration. The agreement was finalized on March 29, 2026. Insilico, listed as HKEX: 3696, is at a clinical stage and utilizes generative technology.
